research.market_summary: S&P 500 reversed from -1% premarket to close +1.74% at ~6,619 after Trump announced a pause on Iran strikes, sending WTI crude down ~7% to ~$91 and Brent to ~$104 from $113+. The rally is fragile: Iran's foreign ministry denied any talks with the US, VIX remains elevated at ~26, and only ~25% of S&P components trade above their 50-day MA — the market is deeply oversold and bouncing on de-escalation hope, not confirmation. Best opportunities lie in AI infrastructure plays with strong independent catalysts (DELL) and oil-de-escalation beneficiaries (UAL), while energy names face near-term headwinds if the truce holds.

DELL.execution_condition: Open at or below $163; intraday price holding above $155 support; no renewed Iran escalation sending risk assets sharply lower in morning session
DELL.reasoning: Dell reported blowout Q4 FY2026 results: $33.4B revenue (+39% YoY), $9B AI server revenue (+342% YoY), and $43B AI backlog — confirming that AI infrastructure demand is real and accelerating regardless of macro uncertainty. The stock has de-risked from ~$190 to ~$160, providing attractive entry for a company guiding $50B in AI server revenue for FY2027. The Trump-driven risk-on bounce today supports technology names, and DELL's fundamental story stands independent of the Iran conflict outcome.
